title: * Professionalism: Maintain a respectful and professional tone.
content:
* Professionalism: Maintain a respectful and professional tone.
* No Personal Conversations: Keep the chat work-related.
* Emergencies Only (for urgent messages): Clearly define what constitutes an "urgent" message that warrants an immediate notification.
* Availability: Set expectations for when people are expected to check the chat (e.g., "first thing in the morning," "throughout the day for urgent updates").
* Create Channels (if applicable):
* #General: For company-wide announcements.
* #Urgent-Issues: For critical, time-sensitive matters that need immediate attention from all relevant parties.
* (Optional) #Job-Updates: For general updates on ongoing jobs, but keep it high-level. Detailed job-specific communication should happen elsewhere (e.g., project management tool).
* Onboarding:
* Training Session: Host a brief meeting to introduce the platform, explain its purpose, and demonstrate how to use it.
* Written Guidelines: Provide a simple, clear document outlining the purpose, etiquette, and how-to for using the group chat.
context: From document: OK, I have been taken in to my father‘s company, Majestic design. It’s… — chunk 14 (chunk 14/22)
